name: Sliding Window
description: Used to perform a required operation on a specific window size of a given array or linked list.
intuition: When a problem asks for the maximum/minimum/longest/shortest something among all contiguous subarrays.
commonMistakes: Not shrinking the window correctly in variable-size sliding window

name: Two Pointers
description: Uses two pointers to iterate through the data structure in tandem until one or both of the pointers hit a certain condition.
intuition: When dealing with sorted arrays (or Linked Lists) and need to find a set of elements that fulfill certain constraints.
commonMistakes: Infinite loops when condition to move pointer isn't met

name: Binary Search
description: Search a sorted array by repeatedly dividing the search interval in half.
intuition: If the array is sorted, or if the search space is monotonic (e.g. true for the first half, false for the second).
whenToUse: Find min/max value satisfying a condition
commonMistakes: Infinite loops due to left <= right vs left < right
Integer overflow when calculating mid: use left + (right - left) / 2
